Level τ: "Amaxophobia"
{{Underline|008000}} {{Class_Variable|banner-text=white|color-l=10%|class=Tau|hexagon=τ|safety=Massive Phobic Hazards|security=Loosened Level Construction|entity=Devoid of Entities}} '''Level τ''' is a sub-level located between [[Level 6]] and [[Level 7]]. ==Description==
[[File:Car Tsunami.png]] An image of '''Level τ''' taken from one of the many skyscrapers.
'''Level τ''' (pronounced "Level Tau", less commonly called "Level 2π") is an incredibly anomalous space within the Backrooms. It can be best described as a combination of a large formerly bustling city and a thick forest, entirely flooded by cars and other similar vehicles, both rusty and new. Navigation without the help of a drone has proven to be significantly troublesome, and as such, most of the level remains undiscovered. The vehicles that flood the majority of the level can vary in size and date of production; this can range from Ford Model T's to modern supercars and electric cars, though the most common vehicles are from the late 90s and 2000s. '''Level τ''''s size can not be properly determined due to how little of the level has been explored. However, the level is linear, meaning that with the right equipment, the level can be explored without getting lost. It is recommended to bring food and water, as much climbing equipment as possible (this includes helmets, gloves, climbing shoes, an icepick or something similar, and carabiners to carry any other equipment one would like to take) and something to keep track of the path which you may take in this level. The level is also reportedly quite cold, with the highest temperatures being around 12°C (53.6°F) and the lowest recorded temperature being around -40°C (-40°F). There is also a 30 hour day and night cycle, but this provides no significant change other than a noticeable change in temperature. At times, it could also rain within the level; one should use this to their advantage as it is one of their only ways to collect safe drinking water outside of looting stores. As of now, no objects or resources related to the Backrooms (like [[Object 1|Almond Water]]) have been found, outside of stores or not; only regular foodstuffs found in [[The Frontrooms]] are known to exist.
[[File:Car Forest.png]] An image of The Forest within '''Level τ'''.
'''Level τ''' is made up of 2 significantly different sections, called The City and The Forest; these 2 sections blend together very frequently, however they are very different in terms of environment and safety. '''The City''' is a sprawling metropolitan city composed of skyscrapers, malls, and other buildings, seemingly abandoned and filled with vehicles; all buildings are barren and beyond repair, with the insides being devoid of any appliances, furniture and other items. '''The City''' has proven to be very troublesome to explore due to the sheer amount of vehicles making any trek across very difficult, since most buildings are detached from each other, forcing wanderers to walk through the vehicles, which could be dangerous if one is not careful. Despite its harsh conditions, it is possible to survive in '''The City''', since some malls which carry foodstuffs have been able to stick out, allowing simple access to a wanderer's needs for food and drink. '''The City''' is usually the first area one will find themselves in, and it is considered more dangerous yet more navigable than The Forest. '''The Forest''' is the second section of '''Level τ'''; it is a large, dense forest composed of many coniferous trees, like spruces and pines; there is significantly less cars within '''The Forest''' than in The City. The terrain is very rough and hilly, making it harder to explore and navigate than The City; however, it should be noted that '''The Forest''' is less hazardous than The City due to there being less sharp objects within one's proximity at any times. Small rivers made of drinkable fresh water are also common, so one should pick some up while they are within '''The Forest'''. Doors are randomly located throughout '''The Forest''', which contain the only known exits out of '''Level τ'''. These doors are usually found upstream, so it is suggested to walk towards the source of a river once you find one. '''The Forest''' is considered by many to be the safer part of '''Level τ''', yet it is also considered the harder one to navigate through. One will be able to tell when they are entering '''The City''' or '''The Forest''' when they stumble upon a space where the 2 sections blend together, leading to a slow decrease in automobiles and most of the environment being overrun with foliage; while not considered its own section, some have began calling this area '''The Overgrown''', implying that this area should be labelled as its own section, or at the very least a sub-section. ==Entities== No entities have been documented in '''Level τ'''. ==Colonies and Outposts== Currently, no colonies or outposts exist within '''Level τ'''; however, plans are being made by the [[M.E.G.]] to begin establishing outposts within both '''The City''' and '''The Forest''' to serve as a rest stop and place to collect resources within The Main Nine, much like [[Level 6.1]]. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *One can get here from [[Level 6]] by simply noclipping - the percentage chance of getting here has not yet been determined, however; *Finding a rusty car floating in the waters of [[Level 7]] and getting in will make one fall unconscious, where they then wake up in '''Level τ'''. *One can find themselves here by opening the door labelled 'τ' and walking through in [[Level 51]]. ===Exits=== *Opening one of the doors in '''The Forest''' will lead one to presumably most safe levels.
